zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor de Vaux於1405年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾特易福德時，她的父親WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton31歲，母親Eleanor Drakelowe31歲。她於1429年與Thomas Giffard在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1478年在她的家鄉享年73歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor de Vaux于1405年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾特易福德时，她的父亲WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton31岁，母亲Eleanor Drakelowe31岁。她于1429年与Thomas Giffard在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1478年在她的家乡享年73岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vaux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of various places in northern France called Vaux, from the Old French plural of val ‘valley’. Compare English Vause and French Devaux .

History: Richard Vaux (1749–90) emigrated from Sussex, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in early youth. Calvert Vaux, landscape architect, emigrated from London, England, to America in 1850 and designed several major US parks, including Central Park in New York City (with Frederick Olmstead). — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant Veaux and altered form Devoe ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Vause
meanings: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vaux (see Vaux ), a common French placename, singular and plural of val ‘valley’, the plural form being Latinized in surnames as de Vallibus. Norman lords with this surname are recorded in 12th- and early 13th-century records in Cumberland (Irthington), Devon (Farwood, in Colyton), East Yorkshire (Warter), Essex (Belchamp), and Leicestershire (Belvoir). Compare Vale .

nickname perhaps from Middle English fals, faus, vaus (Old French fals, faus, Old English fals) ‘deceitful, disloyal, dishonest, mendacious’, but it is more likely the name arises from 1, with the common Anglo-Norman French substitution of le for de in their surname.

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor de Vaux was born in 1405, in Twyford, Buckinghamshire, England, her father, WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton, was 31 and her mother, Eleanor Drakelowe, was 31. She married Thomas Giffard in 1429, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1478, in her hometown, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor de Vaux est née en 1405, à Twyford, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Drakelowe, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Giffard en 1429, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1478, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor de Vaux 1405 in Twyford, Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton 31 und ihre Mutter Eleanor Drakelowe 31. Sie hat 1429 in England Thomas Giffard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1478 im Alter von 73 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Vaux è nata il nel 1405 nel Twyford, Buckinghamshire, England, sua padre, WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Eleanor Drakelowe, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Giffard nel 1429, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1478, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 73 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Vauxが1405年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアトウイフォードで生まれた時，父WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northamptonは31歳，母Eleanor Drakeloweは31歳であった。1429年にイギリスでThomas Giffardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1478年に故郷で73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Vaux(이)가 1405년 영국 벅잉함실르 트w이퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor Drakelowe(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1429년 영국에서 Thomas Giffard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1478년 그녀의 고향에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Vaux nasceu em 1405, em Twyford, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Drakelowe, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Giffard em 1429, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1478, em sua cidade natal, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor de Vaux родилась в 1405 г. в местности твифорд, буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Drakelowe, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Giffard в 1429 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1478 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 73 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eleanor de Vaux nació en 1405, en Twyford, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Eleanor Drakelowe, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Thomas Giffard en 1429, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1478, en su pueblo, a la edad de 73 años.
